_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH) a non-malignant enlargement of the prostate
The procedure is used both for resection and enucleation of prostatic tissue

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
USG with Prostate size and Post Void Volume, Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA), Uroflowmetry report confirming the diagnosis
Proof of hospital registration for holmium laser

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission submitted?
Was the USG with Prostate size and Post Void Volume, Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA), Uroflowmetry report submitted?
Is the hospital registered for holmium laser?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes are missing or do not include evaluation findings or planned line of treatment.
USG with Prostate size, Post Void Volume, Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA), or Uroflowmetry report is missing or does not confirm the diagnosis.
Hospital is not registered or cannot provide proof of registration for holmium laser.
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details are not available at claim stage.
Histopathology report is missing from the claim submission.
Clinical documentation (notes, USG, PSA, Uroflowmetry) is not indicative of Holmium Laser Prostatectomy, leading to a potential query on medical necessity.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary care facilities & registered for holmium laser.
clinical_key_pointers: Holmium Laser Prostatectomy is a minimally invasive treatment for an enlarged prostate.
Also called holmium laser enucleation of the prostate (HoLEP).
This procedure uses laser to remove tissue that is blocking urine flow through the prostate.
Also cuts the prostate tissue into easily removable fragments.
It is a minimally invasive technique that uses a holmium:yttrium-aluminium-garnet (YAG) laser.
Usually Potassium-titanyl-phosphate (KTP) and holmium lasers are used to cut and/or enucleate the prostate; these are widely used laser techniques.
HoLEP uses the excellent incisional and hemostatic properties of the holmium laser wavelength.

Lasers deliver heat to the prostate in various ways, causing tissue death by coagulative necrosis, with subsequent tissue contraction; however, laser coagulation of the prostate in this specific sense has met with limited results.
HoLEP removes the entire portion of the prostate that can block urine flow.
HoLEP also preserves removed tissue that can be examined in the laboratory for investigations.
HoLEP involves four steps: creation of bladder neck incisions, enucleation of the median lobe, enucleation of the lateral lobes off the prostatic capsule, and transurethral morcellation.
additional_information.post_op_care: In most cases, postoperative irrigation is not needed and the catheterization time is less than 24 hours.
